checkpoints.label: Game has 2 or more active sprites.
At least 1 themed backdrop (not the blank white default).
Backdrop matches one of the 3 themes (Eco-Warriors / Smart Cities / Space Pioneers).
Sprites visually relate to the chosen theme (no random off-theme sprites).
At least 1 sprite is original — drawn in Paint Editor, not from Library.
Player sprite is clearly identifiable as the protagonist.
Background is fully coloured (no large white/empty areas).

levels.descriptor: Backdrop & sprites clearly match theme. Original asset(s) in Paint Editor.
Theme recognisable; minor mismatch on one sprite or backdrop.
Partial theme; multiple off-topic or generic elements.
Little to no theme; default assets only.

checkpoints.label: At least one sprite has 2 or more costumes set up.
Next Costume or Switch Costume block is used in the code.
A wait block separates costume changes (animation doesn't flicker).
Costume changes are inside a forever or repeat loop (continuous, not one-shot).
Wait timing produces smooth animation (not too fast, not too slow).
Animation is contextually correct (e.g. only animates while the sprite is moving).

levels.descriptor: ≥1 sprite: Next/Switch Costume + Wait in a loop. Smooth, contextually correct.
Loop present but timing is off or wrong costume indices used.
Costume change exists but missing Wait block or loop structure.
No animation or costume sequencing.

checkpoints.label: Player moves via 'when [key] pressed' OR mouse-pointer tracking.
Movement is responsive — no big lag, no getting stuck on the edge.
Player can reach all four corners of the stage.
At least one 'if touching ___ ?' (sprite or colour) block is present.
Collision triggers a visible reaction (costume change / sound / hide / etc.).
A 'Score' variable is created.
The 'Score' variable shows on the stage.
The 'Score' variable updates when a collision happens.
'Set Score to 0' runs 'when green flag clicked'.

levels.descriptor: Player moves via keyboard/mouse. Collision triggers reaction. Score visible, resets to 0 on ⚑.
Movement & collision both work; one minor bug (e.g. Score doesn't reset).
Movement works; collision absent or non-functional.
Controls broken or no collision logic.

checkpoints.label: The required sprite is added to the project.
Extra Marks: Sprite is originally hand-drawn in the Paint Editor (rather than just taken from the Library).
The sprite matches the visual intent/variant specified in the brief.
The sprite is initially placed at the correct starting coordinates (X/Y) or starting state.

levels.descriptor: Hand-drawn sprite matches intent and starting conditions exactly.
Required sprite added correctly but pulled from library or missing exact starting state.
Sprite added but missing multiple conditions or wrong variant.
Missing or completely wrong sprite.

checkpoints.label: Movement code is present.
Movement uses the exact block type specified (e.g. glide vs move steps).
Movement is enclosed in a loop (forever/repeat).
Loop parameter (if repeat) matches the required count.
Movement speed/timing exactly matches the brief (e.g. wait blocks or glide duration).
Movement does not stall, glitch, or stop unexpectedly.

levels.descriptor: Movement uses exact blocks, timing, and loops flawlessly.
Movement functions but has minor timing/block inaccuracies.
Movement works partially but is missing loop structure or glitchy.
No meaningful movement programmed.

checkpoints.label: Collision detection block (touching) is used.
Collision logic is placed inside an active loop so it can trigger at any time.
Action 1 (e.g. Speech/Think): Triggered correctly.
Action 1: Text exactly matches the brief (capitalisation/punctuation).
Action 2 (e.g. Sound/Costume): Triggered correctly.
Action 2: Exact sound or costume variant is used.
Action 3 (e.g. Score/Stop): Triggered correctly.
Actions happen in the exact sequence requested by the brief.

levels.descriptor: All 3 actions trigger precisely in the correct sequence with exact variants.
Actions trigger but 1-2 parameters or sequencing are slightly off.
Collision detects but only partial actions execute properly.
Collision logic is absent or completely broken.

checkpoints.label: Game has 3 or more active sprites.
Game has 2 or more backdrops.
At least 1 visible scene transition is implemented (not just a static backdrop).
Backdrop matches one of the 3 themes (Eco-Warriors / Smart Cities / Space Pioneers).
Sprites visually relate to the chosen theme.
At least 1 sprite is original (drawn in Paint Editor, not Library).
Overall visual style is consistent (colour palette, line weight, art style).

levels.descriptor: 3+ sprites, 2 backdrops, visible scene transition. Thematically coherent.
Required sprites & backdrops present; transition exists but abrupt.
Some theming; missing 2nd backdrop or scene transition.
Minimal theming; visual requirements not met.

checkpoints.label: A gameplay variable (Score / Lives / Time / etc.) is created.
The variable is visible on the stage.
The variable updates dynamically during gameplay (not static).
Difficulty / progression feels balanced (not trivial, not impossible).
The variable resets correctly when the green flag is clicked.
The variable name is descriptive (not 'variable1' / 'my variable').

levels.descriptor: Variable updates dynamically; balanced difficulty; visible on stage; resets on ⚑.
Variable updates with occasional bugs or incorrect reset.
Variable visible but only partially functional.
Variable missing, static, or never updates.

checkpoints.label: At least 1 'broadcast ___' block is used in the code.
At least 1 'when I receive ___' block matches that broadcast.
The broadcast manages a scene / state transition.
No broken events (a broadcast no-one receives, or vice versa).
The broadcast fires under the correct condition (not on every tick).
Receiver responds exactly as the brief specifies.

levels.descriptor: Broadcast/When I Receive manages ≥1 transition correctly. No broken events.
Structure present; one event fails or receives wrong data.
Broadcast exists but inconsistent or causes duplicate triggers.
No broadcast system or completely broken.

checkpoints.label: At least 1 'My Block' (Custom Block) is defined.
My Blocks group repeated logic (no copy-paste of the same block sequence).
No orphaned scripts (every script starts from a 'when ___' event).
Workspace is reasonably clean (scripts not piled on top of each other).

levels.descriptor: My Blocks group repeated logic. No orphaned scripts. Clean workspace.
Some My Blocks; minor redundancy.
Little organisation; many duplicate scripts.
No optimisation; chaotic workspace.

checkpoints.label: The correct sprite is added per the brief.
A new variable is created.
The variable name matches the brief exactly (case-sensitive).
The variable is visible on the stage.
The variable starting value is initialized correctly (e.g. set to 0 on flag).

levels.descriptor: Correct sprite, exactly-named variable, visible, and initialized correctly.
Variable created but named incorrectly, hidden, or not initialized.
Variable or sprite missing entirely, partial setup only.
No variable setup or completely wrong sprite.

checkpoints.label: A broadcast block is used in the code.
The broadcast message name matches the brief.
The broadcast fires only when the exact required condition is met.
A when I receive block is present to catch the broadcast.
The receiver is placed on the correct target sprite/backdrop.
The receiver responds exactly as specified.

levels.descriptor: Broadcast fired perfectly on condition and received flawlessly.
Broadcast executes but has a wrong name, condition bug, or slightly off response.
Broadcast sent or received but missing the other half or heavily bugged.
Broadcast logic completely absent.

checkpoints.label: The terminal/response condition is detected (e.g. score > 10).
The exact threshold/operator specified in the brief is used.
Action 1 (e.g. Hide/Show): Executes correctly.
Action 2 (e.g. Stop All): Executes correctly.
The sequence runs flawlessly without softlocks or missed triggers.

levels.descriptor: Terminal condition detected exactly, and all actions run flawlessly.
Condition works but action missed or operator slightly wrong.
Condition present but actions do not execute correctly.
No response condition logic programmed.

checkpoints.label: Theme interpretation goes beyond the obvious (e.g. story / narrative arc).
Substantial original assets (paint-edited or vector-drawn, not stock).
Interactive UI elements enhance the narrative (menus / dialogs / hubs).
Theme is consistent across all sprites and backdrops.
Project has at least 1 genuinely unique feature (originality marker).

levels.descriptor: Innovative interpretation; interactive UI enhances narrative; substantial original assets.
Theme clear; some interactive elements present.
Basic theming; limited originality or interaction.
Generic or off-theme; no interactive elements.

checkpoints.label: Scene transitions are smooth (no jarring jumps or hard cuts).
UI buttons are responsive (visible hover / click feedback).
Physics or animation feel polished (no lag, no jitter).
Sound effects or music enhance the experience (not just decoration).
Visible feedback for every player action (sound / costume / movement).
No obvious UX bugs (broken transitions / dead buttons / softlocks).

levels.descriptor: Smooth transitions, responsive UI buttons, polished physics/animation. Professional feel.
Good polish; minor UX issues (e.g. slight lag on transitions).
Functional but rough; limited UI interaction.
Poor UX; broken transitions or non-responsive UI.

checkpoints.label: A List is created in the project.
The List stores at least 1 data type (numbers / strings).
The List is read / queried during gameplay (not just stored).
The List updates dynamically as the game progresses.
At least 1 Scratch Extension is used (Pen / Music / TTS / Video Sensing / etc.).
The Extension is integrated into core gameplay (not decorative).
The Extension works without bugs (no errors / crashes).
The Extension's output is contextually correct for the game state.

levels.descriptor: List stores/retrieves ≥1 data type. Extension fully integrated into gameplay. Zero bugs.
Both present; minor functional bug in one.
One of List or Extension works; the other broken or absent.
Neither List nor Extension functional.

checkpoints.label: Custom Blocks (My Blocks) are defined AND used in the code.
All repetitive logic is in Custom Blocks (no copy-pasted scripts).
Cloning ('create clone of myself') is used somewhere in the project.
Clones are managed properly (deleted when done — no runaway clones).
No orphaned scripts (every script has a 'when ___' entry point).

levels.descriptor: All repetitive logic in Custom Blocks. No orphaned scripts. Cloning used throughout.
Most in Custom Blocks; 1–2 orphaned scripts.
Some Custom Blocks; notable redundancy or one manual duplication.
No Custom Blocks; manual sprite duplication present.

checkpoints.label: The correct sprite(s) are added per the brief.
List or Extension is initialized.
List name / Extension config matches the brief exactly.

levels.descriptor: Sprite added and Extension/List initialized and named perfectly.
Setup done but List named incorrectly or minor Extension config error.
Partial setup, missing List/Extension entirely.
No system setup or completely wrong sprite.

checkpoints.label: Clone/Extension creation logic is present.
Parameter: Coordinates / Placement are exactly as specified.
Parameter: Language / Text Strings are exactly as specified.
Parameter: Loop Count / Iterations are exactly as specified.
Parameter: Timing / Intervals are exactly as specified.
Execution is fully functional with no syntax or logic errors.

levels.descriptor: All logic and all parameters execute exactly per the brief.
Logic works but 1-2 parameters (e.g. coords, loop count) are wrong.
Logic present but buggy or multiple parameters are incorrect.
Clone/Extension logic is missing.

checkpoints.label: The main data action (List update, TTS, clone delete) is present.
The data action processes the exact required data (no hardcoded shortcuts).
Step 1 of the response executes.
Step 2 of the response executes.
The action is consistent and robust across multiple triggers.

levels.descriptor: Action processes correct data and fully executes all response steps.
Action executes but data is hardcoded or a response step is missed.
Action triggers but execution fails or only partial steps run.
No response action programmed.
